debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
mα : MeasurableSpace α
mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω
inst✝⁷ : DecidableEq α
inst✝⁶ : StandardBorelSpace α
inst✝⁵ : Nonempty α
A : ℕ → Ω → α
R : ℕ → Ω → ℝ
P : Measure Ω
inst✝⁴ : IsProbabilityMeasure P
alg : Algorithm α ℝ
ν : Kernel α ℝ
inst✝³ : IsMarkovKernel ν
inst✝² : StandardBorelSpace Ω
inst✝¹ : Nonempty Ω
inst✝ : Countable α
h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P
⊢ iIndepFun (fun p => rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) (P.prod (Bandit.streamMeasure ν))

failed_attempts:
-- rewardByCount matches the stream projection almost surely
  refine iIndepFun_congr (fun p ↦ ?_) ?_
  · exact (condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)).trans
      (condDistrib_ae_eq_cond (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)).symm
  -- The stream variables are independent (projections of a product measure)
  · convert (Measure.iIndepFun_infinitePi_infinitePi ν).comp (fun p ↦ (p.2 + 1, p.1))
      (fun x y h ↦ Prod.ext (Prod.ext_iff.1 h).2 (Prod.ext_iff.1 h).1) using 1

have h_ident : IdentDistrib (fun p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1))
      (fun p ω ↦ ω.2 (p.2 + 1) p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' := h.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream
  exact h_ident.iIndepFun_iff.mpr (by fun_prop)

rw [iIndepFun_sigma_iff]
  exact ⟨fun a ↦ iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h a, IsAlgEnvSeq.iIndepFun_rewardByCount_arms h⟩

let Z (p : α × ℕ) (ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ)) := ω.2 p.2 p.1
  have hZ : iIndepFun Z 𝔓' := by
    rw [iIndepFun_iff_finite_indepFun]
    intro ι s
    let f : (ι → α × ℕ) → (ℕ → α → ℝ) → ι → ℝ := fun x ω i ↦ ω (x i).2 (x i).1
    have : (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ (fun i ↦ Z (s i) ω))
        = (fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ f s ω i)) ∘ Prod.snd := by ext; rfl
    rw [this, Measure.map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)]
    have : (𝔓').snd = Bandit.streamMeasure ν := by
      rw [Measure.snd, Measure.map_fst_prod]; simp
    rw [this]
    exact (iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ Measure.infinitePi ν)).comp (fun i ↦ (s i).2) (by fun_prop)
      |>.comp (fun p ↦ p.2) (by fun_prop) |>.comp (fun p ↦ p.1) (by fun_prop)
      |>.reindex s.subtype_equiv_range.symm
  refine hZ.cong (fun p ↦ ?_)
  -- On the event where the arm is pulled, rewardByCount equals the stream.
  -- Conditioned on stepsUntil, rewardByCount is constant ν p.1, as is the stream.
  -- This implies they are almost everywhere equal.
  rw [Filter.EventuallyEq, ae_iff_of_countable]
  intro n hn
  rw [Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)] at hn
  refine (reward_cond_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) n (by simp) hn).trans ?_
  -- Since Z is independent of stepsUntil, its conditional distribution is its law.
  rw [cond_of_indepFun]
  · exact (hasLaw_Z p.1 p.2).map_eq.symm
  · exact measurableSet_preimage (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)
  · exact (iIndepFun_iff_independent_marginals.1 hZ p).comp (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)

rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp_snd]
  apply iIndepFun_congr (f := fun p ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1)
  · rintro ⟨a, n⟩
    -- rewardByCount corresponds to sampling the stream at the n-th occurrence
    -- In a stationary environment, this is AE equal to the n-th value of the stream
    exact (IsAlgEnvSeq.rewardByCount_eq_eval_stream h a (n + 1)).symm
  · -- The stream values are independent by definition of the stream measure
    rw [Bandit.streamMeasure]
    exact iIndepFun_eval_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ Measure.infinitePi ν)

rw [iIndepFun_iff_measure_inter_preimage_eq_mul]
  intro s
  let f := fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)
  have h_meas (p : α × ℕ) : Measurable (f p) :=
    measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R p.1 (p.2 + 1)
  refine iIndepFun_of_condDistrib_const (fun p ↦ h_meas p) ?_ s
  intro p
  exact condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)

have h_meas (p : α × ℕ) : Measurable (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω) :=
    measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R p.1 (p.2 + 1)
  refine iIndepFun.ae_eq ?_ (fun p ↦ (h.rewardByCount_eq_stream p.1 (p.2 + 1)).symm)
  exact (iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ ν _))).comp
    (fun p ↦ (p.2, p.1))

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  -- The variables `rewardByCount A R a n` are jointly distributed like the stream `Z n a`.
  -- Since `Z` is i.i.d. (product measure), so is `rewardByCount`.
  -- We prove this by showing that for any finite set of indices, the joint law is the product of marginals.
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_finite]
  intro S
  -- The joint distribution matches the product of marginals because the conditional distributions
  -- given the stopping times are constant and equal to `ν`.
  -- This essentially uses `condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil` for each element.
  -- A complete proof would linearize the index set S and use induction or a coupling argument.
  sorry

have h_eq : ∀ p : α × ℕ, rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) =ᵐ[𝔓'] fun ω ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1 :=
    fun p ↦ h.rewardByCount_eq_stream p.1 (p.2 + 1)
  refine iIndepFun_congr h_eq ?_
  exact (Bandit.iIndepFun_stream ν).comp measurable_snd

have h_ident : IdentDistrib (fun ω p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω)
      (fun ω p ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' := h.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream
  refine h_ident.iIndepFun ?_
  exact iIndepFun_infinitePi fun a ↦ iIndepFun_infinitePi fun n ↦ by fun_prop

have h_id : IdentDistrib (fun p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1))
      (fun ω p ↦ ω.2 (p.2 + 1) p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' :=
    h.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream
  refine h_id.symm.iIndepFun ?_
  exact iIndepFun_streamMeasure

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_measure_map_eq]
  · rw [Bandit.streamMeasure]
    have : (𝔓').map (fun ω p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω.1) =
        (Measure.infinitePi fun _ ↦ Measure.infinitePi ν) :=
      h.map_rewardByCount_eq_streamMeasure
    rw [this]
    exact Measure.iIndepFun_infinitePi fun _ ↦ Measure.iIndepFun_infinitePi
  · exact fun p ↦ (measurable_rewardByCount hA hR p.1 (p.2 + 1)).aemeasurable

-- The variables `rewardByCount` are almost everywhere equal to the stream variables
  -- `ω.2 p.2 p.1` in the product space construction. The stream variables are independent
  -- by definition of `Bandit.streamMeasure` as an infinite product.
  convert iIndepFun_snd (μ := P) (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν) (fun p ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1) using 1
  · ext1 p
    -- The fact that `rewardByCount` has the same conditional distribution (constant `ν`)
    -- as the stream variables implies they are equal almost everywhere in this construction.
    -- We use the `HasLaw` and `condDistrib` results to align them.
    refine (ae_eq_of_forall_set_integral_eq_of_sigmaFinite (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) ?_).symm
    intro S hS
    rw [← condDistrib_integral_eq (X := fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω.1)
      (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) hS]
    rw [condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)]
    simp only [Kernel.const_apply, Measure.restrict_apply hS, mul_comm (ν p.1 S)]
    -- The corresponding integral for the stream variable `ω.2 p.2 p.1` yields the same
    -- because it is independent of `stepsUntil` and has marginal `ν p.1`.
    rw [integral_mul_left, Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) hS]
    rw [integral_const, Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) hS]
  · -- The stream variables are independent components of an infinite product measure
    exact iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun a ↦ iIndepFun_infinitePi (ν a))

refine (iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_of_ae_eq (fun p ↦ h.rewardByCount_ae_eq_stream p.1 (p.2 + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero _))).mpr ?_
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp_snd, ← iIndepFun_iff_equiv (Equiv.prodComm α ℕ)]
  exact Bandit.iIndepFun_stream ν

-- We identify the law of the process with the product measure of the marginals.
  -- The marginals are ν p.1, and the product measure is Bandit.streamMeasure ν.
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_hasLaw_range]
  refine ⟨Bandit.streamMeasure ν, ?_⟩
  -- To show the process has the law of the stream, we use the fact that conditional distributions
  -- given the sampling times are exactly the marginals ν a.
  -- This uniqueness property characterizes the i.i.d. product measure.
  refine HasLaw.of_condDistrib_eq_const (fun p ↦ ?_) (fun p ↦ ?_)
  · -- Marginal law
    exact (hasLaw_rewardByCount h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)).map_eq
  · -- Conditional distribution
    exact condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)

let f (p : α × ℕ) (ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ)) := rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω.1
  let g (p : α × ℕ) (ω : ℕ → α → ℝ) := ω p.2 p.1
  have h_ident (p : α × ℕ) : IdentDistrib (f p) (g p) 𝔓' (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
    identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval h p.1 (p.2 + 1) p.2 (by simp)
  have h_meas (p : α × ℕ) : Measurable (f p) :=
    (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R p.1 (p.2 + 1)).comp measurable_fst
  have h_indep : iIndepFun g (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) := by
    rw [Bandit.streamMeasure]
    exact iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ iIndepFun_infinitePi ν)
  exact iIndepFun_of_identDistrib h_meas h_ident h_indep

have h_ident : IdentDistrib (fun p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1))
      (fun p ↦ fun ω ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' :=
    h.identDistrib_rewardByCount_streamMeasure
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_identDistrib_iIndepFun h_ident]
  exact iIndepFun_streamMeasure

rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_sigma]
  refine ⟨fun a ↦ iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h a, ?_⟩
  -- The families of rewards for different arms are independent because they depend on
  -- disjoint subsets of the conditionally i.i.d. reward sequence `R`.
  -- Since `condDistrib R A = ν`, the rewards `R` are independent conditioned on `A`.
  -- The demixing process preserves this independence across arms.
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_finite_indepFun]
  intro S
  have h_law : ∀ a, HasLaw (rewardByCount A R a) (Measure.infinitePi (fun _ ↦ ν a)) 𝔓' :=
    fun a ↦ hasLaw_rewardByCount_seq h a
  have h_indep : ∀ a, iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := fun a ↦ iIndepFun_rewardByCount' h a
  -- We rely on the fact that IsAlgEnvSeq with stationaryEnv implies independence of arms.
  -- This is a standard result for the demixing of bandit rewards.
  sorry -- Proof of independence of arms requires more infrastructure or a specific lemma.

have h_eq : ∀ p : α × ℕ, rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) =ᵐ[𝔓'] fun ω ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1 :=
    fun p ↦ h.rewardByCount_eq_stream p.1 (p.2 + 1)
  refine iIndepFun_congr h_eq ?_
  have h_meas : (𝔓').map Prod.snd = Bandit.streamMeasure ν := by
    rw [Measure.map_snd_prod]
    simp
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp_prod_snd (by fun_prop), h_meas,
    ← iIndepFun_reindex (Equiv.prodComm α ℕ)]
  exact Kernel.iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ Measure.infinitePi ν) _

rw [iIndepFun_iff_ae_eq (fun p ↦ fun ω ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1)]
  · rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp_snd, Bandit.streamMeasure]
    let e := Equiv.curry (α := ℕ) (β := α) (γ := ℝ)
    refine iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp_map.mpr ?_
    convert (iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun (_ : ℕ × α) ↦ ν)).comp Equiv.prodComm _ _ using 1
    · ext p x; simp
    · exact (Measure.pi_pi_map_curry (fun _ ↦ ν)).symm
  · rintro ⟨a, n⟩
    exact h.rewardByCount_eq_stream_apply a n

have h_eq : ∀ p : α × ℕ, rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) =ᵐ[𝔓'] fun ω ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1 :=
    fun ⟨a, n⟩ ↦ IsAlgEnvSeq.rewardByCount_eq_stream h a (n + 1)
  refine iIndepFun.congr ?_ h_eq
  convert iIndepFun_compose_snd (μ := P) (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν)
    (fun (p : α × ℕ) s ↦ s p.2 p.1) (fun _ ↦ trivial) ?_
  · ext1 p ω; rfl
  · rw [Bandit.streamMeasure]
    convert iIndepFun_infinitePi_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ ν) using 1
    ext s p
    rfl

rw [iIndepFun_iff_measure_map_eq_infinitePi]
  · ext1 p
    exact (hasLaw_rewardByCount h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)).map_eq
  · exact measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R
  · sorry -- Independence follows from condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil
exact h.iIndepFun_rewardByCount

have h_ident : IdentDistrib (fun ω p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω.1)
      (fun ω p ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' :=
    IsAlgEnvSeq.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream h
  exact (Measure.iIndepFun_infinitePi (fun _ ↦ Measure.iIndepFun_infinitePi
    (fun _ ↦ ν _))).identDistrib h_ident

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  convert iIndepFun_comp_snd (Bandit.iIndepFun_stream ν) (fun p ↦ (p.2, p.1))
  ext1 p
  exact (IsAlgEnvSeq.rewardByCount_eq_stream h p.1 (p.2 + 1)).symm

rw [iIndepFun_iff_iIndepFun_comp (Denumerable.equivNat (α × ℕ)).symm]
  rw [iIndepFun_nat_iff_forall_indepFun (by fun_prop)]
  intro k
  let p := (Denumerable.equivNat (α × ℕ)).symm k
  apply indepFun_of_condDistrib_const
  exact condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero _)

refine iIndepFun_congr (fun p ↦ rewardByCount_eq_eval_stream h p.1 p.2) ?_
  have : iIndepFun (fun (x : ℕ × α) (ω : ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω x.1 x.2) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
    iIndepFun_eval_eval_streamMeasure ν
  exact (this.comp (fun p ↦ (p.2, p.1))).comp_snd (fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦ ω.2) measurable_snd

have h_ident : IdentDistrib (fun ω p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω.1)
      (fun ω p ↦ ω.2 p.2 p.1) 𝔓' 𝔓' :=
    h.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream
  exact (IdentDistrib.iIndepFun_iff h_ident).2 (Bandit.iIndepFun_stream ν)

refine iIndepFun_congr (fun p ↦ h.rewardByCount_eq_stream p.1 p.2) ?_
  rw [iIndepFun_iff_comp_snd measurable_snd, Measure.map_snd_prod]
  exact Bandit.iIndepFun_streamMeasure_apply ν

refine iIndepFun_of_condDistrib (fun p ↦ ?_)
  exact condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h p.1 (p.2 + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero _)

refine iIndepFun_congr (fun p ↦ h.rewardByCount_ae_eq_stream_apply p.1 (p.2 + 1) (by simp)) ?_
  rw [← iIndepFun_map_iff (by fun_prop) measurable_snd]
  exact Bandit.iIndepFun_stream ν

have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  -- The rewards counted by occurrence are i.i.d. with law ν.
  -- This is a consequence of the stationary environment.
  -- The variables have the same joint distribution as the stream of rewards ω.2.
  exact iIndepFun_of_identDistrib (IsAlgEnvSeq.identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream h)
